subject: Chemistry
q:
Four gases, A, B, C and D have critical temperatures 5.3, 33.2, 126.0 and 154.3K respectively

For their adsorption on a fixed amount of charcoal, the correct order is :
question:
Four gases, A, B, C and D have critical temperatures 5.3, 33.2, 126.0 and 154.3K respectively

For their adsorption on a fixed amount of charcoal, the correct order is :
solution:
Critical temperature of a gas is the temperature at or above which vapor of the gas cannot be liquefied, no matter how much pressure is applied. The extent of adsorption is directly proportional to the critical temperature of the gas. Higher is the critical temperature of the gas, greater is the extent of adsorption.

Order of Tc    D>C>B>A

Order of adsorption D>C>B>A

subject: Chemistry
q: The coagulating power of electrolytes having ions <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Na</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math>,  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Al</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Ba</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> for arsenic sulphide sol increases in the order,
question: The coagulating power of electrolytes having ions <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Na</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math>,  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Al</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Ba</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> for arsenic sulphide sol increases in the order,
solution:
As2S3 /S2- is negatively charged sol.

According to the Hardy Schulze rule, the greater the charge on the cation (oxidation state), the greater is its coagulating power for the negatively charged sol. Hence, the increasing order is:

Na + < Ba 2 + < Al 3 + 

Al 3 +  (highest coagulating power).

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following is a correct statement?
question: Which of the following is a correct statement?
options: Any amount of dispersed phase can be added to emulsion without destabilising it.
Mixing two oppositely charged sols in equal amount neutralises charges and stabilises colloids.
Presence of equal and similar charges on colloidal particles provides stability to the colloidal solution.
solution:
As equal & similar charge particle will repel each other, hence will never precipitate. So, the option D is correct.

Mixing two oppositely charged sols in equal amount neutralises charges and as a result colloids precipitate.

Only a certain amount of dispersed phase can be added to emulsion without destabilising it.

Brownian motion stabilises sols.

subject: Chemistry
q: Ferric chloride is applied to stop bleeding because
question: Ferric chloride is applied to stop bleeding because
options: Blood absorbs <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>FeCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and forms a complex.
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ions coagulate blood which is a negatively charged sol.
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>FeCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> reacts with the constituents of blood which is a positively charged sol.
solution: Ferric chloride is a hemostatic agent, which means that it can be used to stop bleeding by promoting the coagulation of blood. Blood is a colloidal solution of an albuminoid substance and can be coagulated to form clot by applying Fe3+ ion on the open wound. Thus, bleeding caused by a razor during shaving can be treated by rubbing potash alum against the cut which helps in the coagulation of the blood by sealing the blood vessels.

subject: Chemistry
q: If the initial pressure of a gas is 0.03 atm, the mass of the gas adsorbed per gram of the adsorbent is____×10-2 g
question: If the initial pressure of a gas is 0.03 atm, the mass of the gas adsorbed per gram of the adsorbent is____×10-2 g
solution:
Equation for Freundlich's adsorption isotherm is xm=KP1n

logxm=logK+1nlogP

Intercept logK=0.602=log4

K=4

Slope ⇒1n=1 so n=1

so xm=40.03=0.12=12×10-2g

Hence, the mass of the gas adsorbed per gram of the adsorbent is 12×10-2g

subject: Chemistry
q:
The conditions given below are in the context of observing Tyndall effect in colloidal solutions:
(A) The diameter of the colloidal particles is comparable to the wavelength of light used.
(B) The diameter of the colloidal particles is much smaller than the wavelength of light used.
(C) The diameter of the colloidal particles is much larger than the wavelength of light used.
(D) The refractive indices of the dispersed phase and the dispersion medium are comparable.
(E) The dispersed phase has a very different refractive index from the dispersion medium. Choose the most appropriate conditions from the options given below:
question:
The conditions given below are in the context of observing Tyndall effect in colloidal solutions:
(A) The diameter of the colloidal particles is comparable to the wavelength of light used.
(B) The diameter of the colloidal particles is much smaller than the wavelength of light used.
(C) The diameter of the colloidal particles is much larger than the wavelength of light used.
(D) The refractive indices of the dispersed phase and the dispersion medium are comparable.
(E) The dispersed phase has a very different refractive index from the dispersion medium. Choose the most appropriate conditions from the options given below:
solution:
The phenomenon of scattering of light by colloidal particles as a result of which the path of the beam becomes visible is called a tyndall effect. Smaller the diameter and similar the magnitude of refractive indices, lesser is the scattering and hence the tyndall effect and viced-versa. The diameter of the dispersed phase particle should not be smaller than the wavelength of light used because they won't be able to scatter the light so, therefore, the diameter of the dispersed particles should be equal or not much smaller than the wavelength of the light used.
2. The refractive indices (i.e. the ratio of the velocity of light in vacuum to the velocity of light in any medium) of the dispersed phase and the dispersion medium should differ greatly in magnitude than only the particles will be able to scatter the light and tyndall effect will be observed. On the other hand, if the refractive indices of the dispersed phase and dispersion medium are almost similar in magnitude, then there will be no scattering of light and hence, therefore, no tyndall effect is observed.
Hence answer A and E are correct.

question: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>%</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">w</mi><mo>/</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">v</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaCl</mi></math> in water was prepared in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>250</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> beaker. Albumin from the egg was poured into <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaCl</mi></math> solution and stirred well. This resulted in a/ an
solution:
The most appropriate method of making egg-albumin sol is Break an egg carefully and transfer the transparent part of the content to 100 mL of 5% w/V saline solution and stir well. Since the egg-albumin sol can be prepared by stirring only so, it is a lyophilic sol. Since lyophilic sols are easy to prepare while lyophobic sols required some spacial techniques.

Note: egg-albumin is the transparent part of egg. During preparation of sol, water should be cold. If we boil water, the sol will be coagulated.

q: The following statements relate to the adsorption of gases on a solid surface. Identify the incorrect statement among them:
question: The following statements relate to the adsorption of gases on a solid surface. Identify the incorrect statement among them:
options: On adsorption decrease in surface energy appears as heat
On adsorption, the residual forces on the surface are increased
solution:
Adsorption is spontaneous process ∆G is -ve. During adsorption randomness of adsorbate molecules reduced and ∆Stotal is +ve.

∆G=∆H-T∆S

∆H=∆G+T∆S

∆H is highly -ve and residual forces on surface are decreased.

q: Among the following, the correct statement is:
question: Among the following, the correct statement is:
options: sols of metal sulphides are lyophilic.
Brownian movement is more pronounced for smaller particles than for bigger-particles.
one would expect charcoal to adsorb chlorine more than hydrogen sulphide.
Hardy Schulze law states that bigger the size of the ions, the greater is its coagulating power.
solution: Brownian movement is the random motion of particles suspended in a fluid (liquid or gas), resulting from their collision with the fast moving atom or molecules in the liquid or gaseous state of the matter. That means, smaller particles are responsible for the Brownian movement.

subject: Chemistry
q:
CH4 is adsorbed on 1 g charcoal at 0°C following the Freundlich adsorption isotherm. 10.0 mL of CH4 is adsorbed at 100 mm of Hg, whereas 15.0 mL is adsorbed at 200 mm of Hg. The volume of CH4 adsorbed at 300 mm of Hg is 10x mL. The value of x is _________ ×10-2. (Nearest integer)
[Use log102=0.3010, log103=0.4771]
question:
CH4 is adsorbed on 1 g charcoal at 0°C following the Freundlich adsorption isotherm. 10.0 mL of CH4 is adsorbed at 100 mm of Hg, whereas 15.0 mL is adsorbed at 200 mm of Hg. The volume of CH4 adsorbed at 300 mm of Hg is 10x mL. The value of x is _________ ×10-2. (Nearest integer)
[Use log102=0.3010, log103=0.4771]
solution:
xm=Kp1n

x∝V for the same gas
101=K1001n   ...(1)
151=K2001n   ....(2)
V1=K3001n   ...(3)
Divide (2) to (1) =1510=21n
log32=1nlog2
1n=0.4771-0.30100.3010=0.585
Divide (3) to (1) =V10=31n
logV10=1nlog3
logV10=0.585×0.4771=0.2791
V10=100.2791
V=10×100.2791=101.2791=10x
x=1.2791=127.91×10-2≈128×10-2

q: Peptization is a:
question: Peptization is a:
options: Process of converting a colloidal solution into precipitate
Process of converting soluble particles to form colloidal solution
Process of bringing colloidal molecule into solution
Process of converting precipitate into colloidal solution
solution: The process of converting a precipitate into colloidal solution by shaking it with dispersion medium in the presence of small amount of electrolyte. The electrolyte used is called peptizing agent.

q: The most appropriate method of making egg-albumin sol is:
question: The most appropriate method of making egg-albumin sol is:
options: Break an egg carefully and transfer the transparent part of the content to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math><mn>5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">%</mi></math> w/V saline solution and stir well

Keep the egg in boiling water for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> minutes. After removing the shell, transfer the yellow part of the content to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math><mn>5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">%</mi></math> w/V saline solution and homogenize with a mechanical shaker

Keep the egg in boiling water for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> minutes. After removing the shell, transfer the white part of the content to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math><mn>5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">%</mi></math> w/V saline solution and homogenize with a mechanical shaker

Break an egg carefully and transfer only the yellow part of the content to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math><mn>5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">%</mi></math> w/V saline solution and stir well
solution:
Preparation of egg albumin sol. is done following two steps.

(i) Prepare100 mL of 5% (w/V)  solution of NaCl  in water in a 250 mL beaker.
(ii) Break one egg in a porcelain dish and pipette out the albumin and pour it in sodium chloride solution. Stir well to ensure the preparation of sol.

subject: Chemistry
q:
42.12% (w/v) solution of NaCl causes precipitation of a certain sol in 10 hours. The coagulating value of NaCl for the sol is
[Given : Molar mass : Na=23.0 g mol-1; Cl=35.5 g mol-1]
question:
42.12% (w/v) solution of NaCl causes precipitation of a certain sol in 10 hours. The coagulating value of NaCl for the sol is
[Given : Molar mass : Na=23.0 g mol-1; Cl=35.5 g mol-1]
solution:
The minimum concentration of an electrolyte in millimoles per litre required to cause precipitation of a sol in two hours is called coagulating value.

42.12 % means 100 ml solution contain 42.12 g of salt.moles of salt = 42.1258.5 = 0.72 moles = 720 milimoles

Since 100 ml required 720 milimoles

therefor 1 L will required 7200 milimoles.

It will coagulate in 10 hours so for 2 hours = 5×7200 = 14400 mili mol L-1

Note: NTA has either made mistake in question data or in options.

q: Which of the following is used for the preparation of colloids ?
question: Which of the following is used for the preparation of colloids ?
solution:
Bredig’s Arc Method is used for preparation of colloidal sol’s of less reactive metal like Au, Ag, Pt.

Ostwald process is used for preparation of nitric acid.

Van Arkel method is used for refining Zirconium or Titanium.

The Mond process is a method for refining nickel. The nickel oxide and impurities react with the hydrogen gas to form an impure solid nickel.

subject: Chemistry
q: Incorrect statement for Tyndall effect is
question: Incorrect statement for Tyndall effect is
options: The refractive indices of the dispersed phase and the dispersion medium differ greatly in magnitude.
The diameter of the dispersed particles is much smaller than the wavelength of the light used.
It is used to distinguish a true solution from a colloidal solution.
During projection of movies in the cinemas hall, Tyndall effect is noticed.
solution:
Tyndall effect is observed only when the following two conditions are satisfied.

(i) The diameter of the dispersed particles is not much smaller than the wavelength of the light used; and

(ii) The refractive indices of the dispersed phase and the dispersion medium differ greatly in magnitude.

This is a characteristic property of the colloid solutions.

subject: Chemistry
q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo> </mo><mi>NaCl</mi></math> is required to coagulate <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>As</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> solution in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math> hours. The coagulating value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaCl</mi></math> is ___________.
question: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo> </mo><mi>NaCl</mi></math> is required to coagulate <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>As</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> solution in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn></math> hours. The coagulating value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaCl</mi></math> is ___________.
solution:
Coagulating value =Millimoles of electrolyteVolume of sol in litre

20 ml of 0.5 M NaCl contains NaCl=20×0.5=10 milli moles

200 ml of As2S3 required NaCl for the coagulation =10 milli moles

∴ 1000 ml of As2S3 required NaCl for the coagulation =10×1000/200=50 milli moles.

solution: Due to the bigger size of colloidal particles the number of particles in a colloidal solution is comparatively small as compared to a true solution. Hence the values of colligative properties are of small order as compared to values shown by true solutions at same concentration.

subject: Chemistry
q: Adsorption of a gas follows Freudlich adsorption isotherm. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is the mass of the gas adsorbed on mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> of the adsorbent, the correct plot of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mfrac></math> versus p is:
question: Adsorption of a gas follows Freudlich adsorption isotherm. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is the mass of the gas adsorbed on mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> of the adsorbent, the correct plot of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mfrac></math> versus p is:
solution:
From Freundlich adsorption isotherm
xm∝P (At low pressure)
xm∝P° (At high pressure)
→ On increasing temperature physical adsorption decreases.
